model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size should expose the model to more unique training examples within the same number of optimizer updates, which can reduce validation loss in this undertrained regime.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The previous gain suggests this setup is strongly data-limited under fixed steps; increasing batch size again should expose even more unique examples within the same 256 updates, potentially lowering validation loss further.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After scaling batch size up, increasing learning rate is a standard paired adjustment that can improve optimization speed within the same fixed number of steps.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Validation loss is still dropping with stronger optimization, so a modest capacity increase is a logical next step; raising base channels from 12 to 16 should improve representation power while keeping the architecture simple.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The model is still improving with added capacity, and the current head is a narrow bottleneck; increasing hidden width in the classifier is a low-risk way to add expressiveness without changing training dynamics much.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With larger batch and capacity, switching to AdamW is a targeted optimizer-family change that often improves generalization via decoupled weight decay while preserving similar optimization behavior.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW produced a small regression at this budget, so reverting to Adam is the safest optimization-family move to return to the better-performing optimizer before further tuning.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)","new":"    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 20)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With only 256 total steps and already stable training, the current 10% warmup may be too conservative; shortening warmup should let the model spend more of the budget near peak learning rate.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 20)","new":"    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The shorter warmup regressed validation loss, so reverting to the previous 10% warmup restores the better-performing schedule setting.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After increasing model capacity, a modestly stronger weight decay can improve generalization with minimal complexity and without changing the core optimization setup.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher weight decay regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1e-4 restores the previously best regularization setting before exploring other directions.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With the current larger batch and stable schedule, a small learning-rate increase is a focused next probe that may improve fit within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last learning-rate increase improved loss, so a further small step up is a high-value continuation to test whether the optimum is still at a higher LR in this fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss has improved consistently as learning rate increased, so one more incremental LR increase is the most direct high-upside test with remaining budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The strongest recent gains are coming from incremental learning-rate increases, so testing 4e-3 is the highest-probability next improvement under the remaining step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Increasing to 4e-3 regressed from the best observed setting, so reverting to 3.5e-3 restores the strongest learning-rate value found so far.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With learning-rate tuning near a local optimum, a small classifier-width increase is a simple capacity bump that may further reduce loss without changing the training loop.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing classifier width to 128 caused a regression, so reverting to 96 restores the best-performing capacity setting before the final step.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.25e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With 3.5e-3 better than 3e-3 but 4e-3 worse, a small interpolation toward the interior (3.25e-3) is a final high-probability probe for a local LR optimum.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}
